summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on April 26, 2022. The board heard an announcement that the township park has been officially transferred to Clermont County Park District. Two zoning cases were heard and approved: a B-2 to PDM rezoning for a landscape business at 1591 State Route 28, and a B-2 to PDM rezoning for warehouse/product assembly buildings at 1636 State Route 28. A noise complaint from residents near State Route 28 regarding a landscaping company operating late at night was also raised during community forum.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills (April 13-26, 2022) passed unanimously
Zoning case ZC-2022-001 (1591 State Route 28) approved with staff recommendations and PDM zoning designation instead of M-1
Zoning case ZC-2022-002 (1636 State Route 28) approved with conditions as submitted under PDM zoning
Motion to adjourn at 7:55 PM passed
action_items: Director Holbert to work with Dale and Victoria Sweet regarding noise complaint from business near 1785 State Route 28
Harry Holbert to send out notices and information on proposed Curb and Linton subdivision developments to zoning commission and then board of trustees
Jessica Holmes/Park Board to finalize fiscal year-end matters related to park transfer to Clermont County
Clermont Capital to issue public relations announcement regarding park transfer
Mears Tucker (1591 SR 28) to obtain formal maintenance agreement with shared driveway property owners after property closing
Daniel Mac (1636 SR 28) to submit updated architect plans with 5-foot stone veneer, landscaping plan, green space notation, and apply for building permits
Board to review Local Government Fund distribution formula paperwork ahead of May Township Association meeting
key_amounts: 1591 SR 28 existing building: approximately 3,258 square feet (600 sq ft office, 2,200 sq ft garage/storage)
1636 SR 28: two buildings each 6,720 square feet for combined 13,340 square feet (note: transcript figures slightly inconsistent)
1636 SR 28 impervious surface ratio: existing 9.4%, proposed 16.7% (code allows up to 75%)
1636 SR 28: 10 total parking spaces proposed (5 per building)
10% minimum green space preservation required for 1636 SR 28
notable_topics: Transfer of Goshen Township park to Clermont County Park District
Zoning case ZC-2022-001: B-2 to PDM rezoning for landscape business at 1591 State Route 28
Zoning case ZC-2022-002: B-2 to PDM rezoning for warehouse/product assembly at 1636 State Route 28
Noise complaint from residents near State Route 28 about late-night business operations
Local Government Fund distribution negotiations with villages, cities, and townships
Clermont County Planning Commission recommendations on proposed Curb and Linton subdivision developments
Overburning issues in the township being addressed via social media and website
Recent CPR save by fire department
notes: The existing building at 1636 State Route 28 was noted as having been approved in error previously, requiring the current zone change to correct the oversight. The applicant at 1591 State Route 28 had an address correction from a non-legal street name (Pets Avenue) to State Route 28. The chairperson emphasized rules about no commentary from the audience during zoning hearings, referencing issues from a prior meeting. A trustee birthday was acknowledged. The adjournment roll call mentioned a 'Trustee Croninger' which appears to be an error in the transcript, likely referring to one of the three seated trustees.
title: Goshen Twp. BOT 4/26/2022
